title: eremophila pungens
intro: Eremophila pungens, also known as the alpine emu bush, is a shrub native to Australia that belongs to the family Scrophulariaceae. The medicinal properties of Eremophila pungens are attributed to its rich content of iridoid glycosides, which have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions. The plant is said to have anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, providing several health benefits, including relief from colds, flu, and respiratory issues, as well as aiding in wound healing and skin conditions. Eremophila pungens can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, and its extracts can also be found in topical creams and ointments.
